title: Hyperthreading
summary: Describes hyperthreading: one physical processor appearing as two virtual processors, duplicating state components while sharing execution resources.
content_points: Duplicated: general-purpose registers, control registers, interrupt controller, status registers.
Shared: execution units, branch prediction units, buses, control unit.
When one virtual processor stalls, the other can use the pipeline stages.
Not equal to a second physical CPU: shared resources may bottleneck.
Low hardware cost: only state components duplicated.
learning_objectives: Explain how hyperthreading creates virtual processors
Describe which components are duplicated vs. shared
Assess the performance benefit vs. adding a physical CPU
